case_title: REBECCA BUNTING VS. THE REALREAL, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION, ET AL

docket_entries.description: NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
NOTICE OF REMOTE APPEARANCE (TRANSACTION ID # 77461558) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.
ORDER RE: COMPLEX CASE DESIGNATION - DENIED

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT SUA SPONTE GRANTS RECONSIDERATION OF THE NOVEMBER 18, 2025 ORDER AND DENIES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION AS MOOT. THE HEARING ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY THIS ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION IS RESET TO APRIL 30, 2026. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE AN OPPOSITION BY NO LATER THAN NINE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. DEFENDANT MAY ELECT TO PROCEED ON ITS ALREADY-FILED REPLY BRIEF OR MAY FILE A NEW REPLY BRIEF BY NO LATER THAN FIVE COURT DAYS IN ADVANCE OF THE CONTINUED HEARING DATE. SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED
LODGING OF EXHIBITS IN SUPPORT OF RESPONSE TO ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE (TRANSACTION ID # 78955947) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BUNTING, REBECCA

301, DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION, TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON APR-30-2026, IS CONTINUED FOR HEARING TO MAY-13-2026, 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 301. SEE ORDER FILED THIS DATE. JUDGE CHRISTINE VAN AKEN

LAW AND MOTION 301, HEARING ON DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (OCT-16-2025 FILING, RE-SET PER THE COURT'S MAR-24-2026 RULING). THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY IS GRANTED. SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (Oct-16-2025 FILING, RE-SET PER THE COURT'S MAR-24-2026 RULING)
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE REALREAL, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION (Oct-16-2025 FILING, RE-SET PER THE COURT'S MAR-24-2026 RULING)

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Rebecca Bunting, in pro per, Plaintiff.
Matthew J. Adler, Esq., of Faegre Drinker Biddle & Reath LLP, (419) 591-7500, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is GRANTED.

***See Word minutes for complete ruling***

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-24-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION

Appearances via Zoom:
Rebecca Bunting (360-402-5179, sharalottathings@outlook.com) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona (appears at 9:11 am);
Matthew J. Adler of Faegre Drinker Biddle & Reath LLP (415-591-7671, matthew.adler@faegredrinker.com) for defendant and opposing party

The matter is called at 9:07 am. There is no appearance by plaintiff. The court hears arguments on behalf of defendant.

Plaintiff joins the video conference at 9:11 am. The court summarizes the argument so far and continues to hear from the parties.

Arguments having been concluded, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff has moved under Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 473, subdivision (d), to vacate this court's November 18, 2025 order granting defendant's motion to compel arbitration. The court sua sponte grants reconsideration of the November 18, 2025 order because the court administratively erred in overlooking plaintiff's "Supplemental Declaration" and exhibits, which should have been construed as an opposition to the motion. The court denies plaintiff's motion as moot.

The hearing on defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay this action pending arbitration is reset to April 30, 2026. Plaintiff may file an opposition by no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Defendant may elect to proceed on its already-filed reply brief or may file a new reply brief by no later than five court days in advance of the continued hearing date. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
